pr.title: >50% fuzzy match on all remaining item TUs
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Large item-decomp progress PR targeting previously low-fuzzy item translation units, with header type recovery and many item source implementations. The author explicitly says Claude generated much of the initial work and that it required multi-day human cleanup. Bot report evidence claims +13772 matched-code bytes, +328 matched-data bytes, 54 new matches, and 47 unmatched-item improvements. Review feedback focused on include style and not deferring an sdata2 TODO.

what_changed: Added or improved implementations across many remaining low-fuzzy item TUs, especially itseakneedlethrown, itdosei, itmewtwoshadowball, itunknown, itpikachutjoltair, itkirbycutterbeam, ithitodeman, itzgshell, itgreatfoxlaser, itlipstickspore, itclinkmilk, itpatapata, itklap, itclimbersstring, and itkyasarin.
Recovered or refined item variable and attribute structs in itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h, replacing some generic padding or unrelated union-arm access with more item-specific fields.
Updated it_80279C48 to take only Item_GObj*, updated its header declaration, and cleaned up a Chicorita callsite accordingly.
Changed two logic table entries to use it_803F6F50.states rather than it_803F6F50 directly.
Small fighter-side changes adjusted a Sheik needle helper call and a comparison against it_802EAF28.
Bot report claims 54 new matches and 47 improvements, with matched code increasing to 63.04% and matched data to 38.13%.
smart_moves: Recovered typed item-var union arms for items such as climbersstring and clinkmilk instead of continuing to borrow unrelated arms such as bombhei, gamewatchchef, or it_266F.
Kept helper signature truthfulness aligned by changing it_80279C48 in both source and header, then updating at least one callsite.
Used header struct expansion to support multiple item implementations rather than relying only on raw offsets in each C file.
The author explicitly documented AI-assisted generation and human cleanup, which is useful context for reviewers and future curators assessing the risk profile of broad fuzzy-match PRs.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Broad AI-assisted fuzzy matching can produce substantial progress, but the PR evidence frames it as cleanup-heavy and reviewer-sensitive rather than a substitute for human decomp review.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Claude was used on all <50% item TUs and then cleaned up over several days; human is still in the loop

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering item code, update item-specific union arms and shared headers as soon as the body evidence supports the fields; this avoids borrowing unrelated item vars in implementation code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itclimbersstring replaced it_266F and bombhei field accesses with climbersstring
diff_excerpt: itclinkmilk replaced gamewatchchef access with clinkmilk
diff_excerpt: itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h added/expanded item-var structs

decomp_lessons.lesson: Function signature cleanup should propagate to the owning header and all affected callsites in the same PR slice.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: it_80279C48 body changed to one argument

decomp_lessons.lesson: Reviewers may flag generated include forms even in otherwise matching-focused PRs; include paths and angle/quote style are reviewable source quality, not incidental formatting.

naming_conventions.convention: Item-var structs added in headers follow item-specific names such as itCLinkMilk_ItemVars, itClimbersString_ItemVars, itKirby2F23_ItemVars, and itMewtwoShadowball_DatAttrs.
Some semantic helper names were introduced for local inline cleanup, such as itDosei_SetFacingAngle, itDosei_SetSpeed, itCLinkMilk_NotifyParent, and itCLinkMilk_Destroy.
Many unresolved functions retained address-style names such as it_802C27D4, it_802C3864, it_802C3950, and it_802C8B28 where stronger semantics were not shown in the PR slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK in several recovered item functions to preserve stack/codegen shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Loaded evidence shows tactic use but not narrow objdiff rationale per function.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced small static inline helpers around repeated JObj/item operations, including rotation, speed, notification, and destroy helpers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Useful cleanup candidate, but PR slice does not prove which inline forms were required for exact matching.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used typed item-var fields to express offsets in item-specific structs, including Vec3 and HSD_GObj fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Type recovery is well evidenced by diff, but exact correctness of each field is not independently validated by loaded excerpts.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjusted source expressions for codegen/fuzzy matching, such as dropping an unused assignment result and using 0U instead of NULL in one comparison.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Evidence supports edits, but no reviewer explanation or local objdiff excerpt is loaded for these micro-tactics.

review_feedback.feedback: Use #include <baseline/gobj.h> rather than the added quoted baselib/gobj include form.
Questioned deferring an sdata2 placement TODO for it_804DC878: 'Is there a reason not to do it now?'
Same include-style issue for gobj include.
Assuming the intended header is MSL/math.h, use #include <MSL/math.h>.
Apply the same math include-style correction as prior comment.
Fix include style as in previous math include comment.

follow_up_queries: Claude AI-assisted decomp fuzzy item TUs

notes: Raw diff is truncated, so detailed per-function changes for all 38 files are not fully visible in loaded evidence.
Bot report provides match/improvement counts, but loaded excerpts do not include per-function objdiff logs or build commands.
PR body admits uncertainty about whether every item TU crossed 50%, so the intake should not assert that all remaining item TUs actually exceeded 50%.
Review comments show requested include/style changes but loaded evidence does not show whether each comment was resolved before merge.
Field and struct additions are visible, but the loaded slice does not independently validate every recovered field type or offset beyond the diff.

accepted_candidate_records.title: AI-assisted broad fuzzy matching needs explicit human cleanup and review scrutiny
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2409 is useful historical evidence that AI-generated decomp output can move many item TUs but should be treated as cleanup-heavy; the author explicitly described Claude output as messy and emphasized human-in-the-loop cleanup.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover item-specific union arms when item bodies expose fields
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR replaced unrelated item-var union arm usage with item-specific arms in visible item code and added corresponding header structs, supporting the practice of moving from borrowed fields to typed item vars during item decomp.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Include style is reviewed even in matching-heavy item PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: Multiple PR #2409 review comments asked for specific include forms such as <baseline/gobj.h> and <MSL/math.h>, showing that generated include paths should be normalized during cleanup.

source_update_candidates.title: Normalize generated include paths during cleanup
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding or cross-linking a standard that generated includes should use project-preferred canonical include forms; PR #2409 reviewer feedback specifically requested <baseline/gobj.h> for gobj and <MSL/math.h> for math rather than quoted or generic math include forms.
source_update_candidates.reason: Current loaded standards mention established include style in truthful-headers-and-includes, but this PR provides concrete historical review evidence for canonical gobj/math include forms.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not leave obvious section-placement TODOs without justification
source_update_candidates.text: Consider a small note under data-sections or verification standards: if a PR introduces a known section-placement issue such as a symbol marked 'TODO: needs to be in .sdata2', reviewers may expect it to be fixed or justified immediately.
source_update_candidates.reason: Reviewer asked 'Is there a reason not to do it now?' on a new it_804DC878 .sdata2 TODO, but this is one comment and should be curator-reviewed before generalizing.

source_update_candidates.title: Item item-var type recovery often lands in itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2409 added or refined item variable and attribute structs in src/melee/it/itCharItems.h and src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h while implementing many src/melee/it/items/*.c functions.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scope hint for future item decomp, but should be checked against current source organization before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not record that all remaining item TUs exceeded 50%; the author explicitly said they were not sure.
Do not record each bot-reported function as accepted matched ground truth from this intake alone; the slice includes a bot summary, not full objdiff/build logs.
Do not infer that Claude output is acceptable as-is; PR body says it required cleanup and review comments show remaining issues.
Do not promote every recovered struct field name/type as independently validated; the diff shows additions but not the full verification context.
